            </div></td></tr><tr><th scope="row" class="infobox-label">Location</th><td class="infobox-data label"><a href="/wiki/Liberia" title="Liberia">Liberia</a> - <a href="/wiki/Ivory_Coast" title="Ivory Coast">Ivory Coast</a> – <a href="/wiki/Guinea" title="Guinea">Guinea</a></td></tr><tr><th scope="row" class="infobox-label"><a href="/wiki/Mountain_range" title="Mountain range"><span class="nowrap">Parent range</span></a></th><td class="infobox-data category"><a href="/wiki/Nimba_Range" title="Nimba Range">Nimba Range</a></td></tr></tbody></table>
<p><b>Mount Richard-Molard</b>, also known as <b>Mount Nimba</b> (<a href="/wiki/French_language" title="French language">French</a>: <i lang="fr">Mont Nimba</i>) is a mountain along the border of <a href="/wiki/Guinea" title="Guinea">Guinea</a>, <a href="/wiki/Ivory_Coast" title="Ivory Coast">Ivory Coast</a> and <a href="/wiki/Liberia" title="Liberia">Liberia</a> in <a href="/wiki/West_Africa" title="West Africa">West Africa</a>. The summit is on the border of <a href="/wiki/Guinea" title="Guinea">Guinea</a> and <a href="/wiki/Ivory_Coast" title="Ivory Coast">Ivory Coast</a> and at 1,752 m (5,748 ft) is the highest peak for both countries. The mountain is part of the <a href="/wiki/Guinea_Highlands" title="Guinea Highlands">Guinea Highlands</a>, which straddles the borders between the three countries. The nearest major settlements are the town <a href="/wiki/Yekepa" title="Yekepa">Yekepa</a> in Liberia and the towns of <a href="/wiki/Bossou" title="Bossou">Bossou</a> and <a href="/wiki/N%27Zoo" title="N'Zoo">N'Zoo</a> in Guinea.<sup id="cite_ref-voa2008_2-0" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-voa2008-2"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>2<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p>
<meta property="mw:PageProp/toc">
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Toponymy">Toponymy</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Mount_Richard-Molard&action=edit&section=1" title="Edit section: Toponymy"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p>The mountain is named after the French geographer Jacques Richard-Molard, who died in an accident at the mountain site in 1951.<sup id="cite_ref-3" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-3"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>3<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Before that it was called <b>Mount Nouon</b>.<sup id="cite_ref-Pugh1969_4-0" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-Pugh1969-4"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>4<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Geology">Geology</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Mount_Richard-Molard&action=edit&section=2" title="Edit section: Geology"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p>The mountain is rich in <a href="/wiki/Iron_ore" title="Iron ore">iron ore</a> and <a href="/wiki/Cobalt" title="Cobalt">cobalt</a>. The mix of iron rich <a href="/wiki/Quartzite" title="Quartzite">quartzite</a> sheets, <a href="/wiki/Schist" title="Schist">schists</a> and <a href="/wiki/Granite" title="Granite">granite</a> <a href="/wiki/Gneiss" title="Gneiss">gneiss</a> have characterised the geological <a href="/wiki/Pedogenesis" class="mw-redirect" title="Pedogenesis">pedogenesis</a>.<sup class="noprint Inline-Template Template-Fact" style="white-space:nowrap;">[<i><a href="/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed" title="Wikipedia:Citation needed"><span title="This claim needs references to reliable sources. (February 2018)">citation needed</span></a></i>]</sup>
</p>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Conservation">Conservation</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Mount_Richard-Molard&action=edit&section=3" title="Edit section: Conservation"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p>Mount Richard-Molard lies within the <a href="/wiki/Mount_Nimba_Strict_Nature_Reserve" title="Mount Nimba Strict Nature Reserve">Mount Nimba Strict Nature Reserve</a>, which currently covers 17,540 hectares and straddles the borders of <a href="/wiki/Guinea" title="Guinea">Guinea</a> and <a href="/wiki/Ivory_Coast" title="Ivory Coast">Ivory Coast</a>.<sup class="noprint Inline-Template Template-Fact" style="white-space:nowrap;">[<i><a href="/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed" title="Wikipedia:Citation needed"><span title="This claim needs references to reliable sources. (February 2018)">citation needed</span></a></i>]</sup><sup id="cite_ref-5" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-5"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>5<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="World_Heritage_status">World Heritage status</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Mount_Richard-Molard&action=edit&section=4" title="Edit section: World Heritage status"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p><a href="/wiki/Mount_Nimba_Strict_Nature_Reserve" title="Mount Nimba Strict Nature Reserve">Mount Nimba Strict Nature Reserve</a> is a site with outstanding universal value and was therefore nominated to the <a href="/wiki/World_Heritage_List" class="mw-redirect" title="World Heritage List">World Heritage List</a> in 1981.<sup id="cite_ref-6" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-6"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>6<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> It has been listed as <a href="/wiki/List_of_World_Heritage_in_Danger" title="List of World Heritage in Danger">World Heritage in Danger</a> since 1992.
</p><p>This site was added to Guinea's "Tentative List" for consideration for <a href="/wiki/UNESCO" title="UNESCO">UNESCO</a> <a href="/wiki/World_Heritage_Site" title="World Heritage Site">World Heritage Site</a> status on 29 March 2001, in the cultural category.<sup id="cite_ref-7" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-7"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>7<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p><p><a href="/wiki/Mount_Nimba_Strict_Nature_Reserve" title="Mount Nimba Strict Nature Reserve">Mount Nimba Strict Nature Reserve</a> has been listed as a World Heritage Site in both Guinea and Ivory Coast, under the criteria for natural heritage.<sup id="cite_ref-8" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-8"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>8<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p>
